column4: User Story
column7: Acceptance Criteria
column9: Dependencies
column10: Risks & Mitigations

column4: N/A
column7: N/A

column4: As a new user, I want to register an account with my email and password, so that I can access features.
column7:
1.        Given the user provides a valid, unused email and a password that meets security requirements, When they submit the registration form, Then the system creates the account and sends a verification email.
2.        Given the user provides an email that is already registered, When they attempt to submit the form, Then the system displays an error message stating the email is already in use.
3.        Given the user provides a weak password (e.g., missing length or complexity requirements), When they attempt to submit, Then the system displays a clear error message explaining the requirement.
4.        Given the user receives a verification email, When they click the verification link within the valid time frame, Then their account becomes verified and they are prompted to log in.
column9:
•        Email service provider (for verification emails).
•        Database or authentication service (e.g., Firebase Auth or backend API) for credential storage.
 •        Validation logic for email and password requirements.
column10:
•        Risk: Verification emails may be blocked or delayed → Mitigation: Implement resend option and log delivery errors.
•        Risk: Weak password validation could expose security risks → Mitigation: Define password policy clearly and unit-test it.
•        Risk: Duplicate registration attempts → Mitigation: Handle race conditions with unique email constraints in DB.

column4: As a returning user, I want to log in with my email and password, so that I can access my account.
column7:
1.        Given a registered and verified email with a correct password, When the user submits the login form, Then they are redirected to the dashboard within 2 seconds.
2.        Given incorrect login credentials, When the user attempts to log in, Then the system displays an error “Email or password is incorrect” without specifying which field is wrong.
3.        Given 5 consecutive failed login attempts, When the user tries again, Then the system enforces a 15-minute lockout.
4.        Given an authenticated session, When 30 minutes pass without activity, Then the session expires and the user is required to re-authenticate.
column9:
•        Session/token management system.
•        Backend authentication service.
•        Account verification from “Register Account.”
column10:
•        Risk: Timing of dashboard redirect might exceed 2 seconds due to backend latency → Mitigation: Add loading state or async indicator.
•        Risk: Lockout feature could frustrate users → Mitigation: Provide clear UI feedback and password reset link.
•        Risk: Session timeout not syncing across tabs → Mitigation: Implement shared session logic using storage events.

column4: As a user who forgot a password, I want to reset it via email, so that I can regain account access.
column7:
1.        Given a user on the login page, When they click “Forgot password?”, Then a form appears requesting their registered email.
2.        Given a valid registered email, When the user submits the form, Then a reset link is sent that expires in 30 minutes and can be used once.
3.        Given an unregistered email, When the user submits it, Then the system still confirms that an email was sent without revealing account existence.
4.        Given the user clicks the reset link within 30 minutes, When they create a new valid password, Then the system updates their credentials and prompts login.
column9:
•        Email service provider (shared with registration).
•        Secure token generation for one-time reset links.
•        Password validation logic from registration.
column10:
•        Risk: Reset link reuse or token theft → Mitigation: Token invalidation upon use.
•        Risk: Email delivery failure → Mitigation: Retry mechanism and optional support contact.
•        Risk: Weak new password → Mitigation: Apply same password policy validation as registration.

column4: As a user who no longer wants to use the services, I want to permanently delete my account so that my personal data is erased and no longer stored.
column7:
1.        Given an authenticated user, When they navigate to the settings page, Then an option to permanently delete the account is visible.
2.        Given a user chooses to delete, When they confirm via password re-entry or confirmation dialog, Then the system permanently deletes the account and all personal data.
3.        Given a successful deletion, When the process completes, Then the user receives a confirmation message or email and all active sessions are terminated.
4.        Given the account has been deleted, When the user attempts to log in again, Then the system prevents access.
5.        Given non-personal analytics data exist, When the account is deleted, Then such data remain but are fully anonymized.
column9:
•        Authentication system and user database.
•        Data retention policies and legal compliance (e.g., GDPR).
•        Email service (confirmation message).
column10:
•        Risk: Irreversible deletion could lead to accidental data loss → Mitigation: Double confirmation step and optional grace-period queue.
•        Risk: Incomplete deletion (e.g., orphaned data in logs) → Mitigation: Implement cascade delete and verify through QA scripts.
•        Risk: Data anonymization may be insufficient → Mitigation: Audit anonymization methods and maintain compliance documentation.

column4: N/A
column7: N/A

column4: As a user, I want the system to fetch articles from multiple news sources so that I can access diverse perspectives.
column7:
1.        Given the system is configured with at least three external sources (via API or RSS), When a fetch cycle runs, Then the system retrieves the latest articles from all configured sources.
2.        Given the fetch interval is 15 minutes, When the next interval elapses, Then the system automatically triggers a background fetch without manual input.
3.        Given a fetch failure occurs (e.g., timeout, invalid response), When the system processes results, Then an error log entry is recorded with the source name and timestamp.
column9:
•        API keys or RSS feed URLs for external news sources.
•        Scheduler or background job runner (e.g., Cron, Celery, Cloud Function).
•        Logging/monitoring service (e.g., CloudWatch, ELK stack).
column10:
•        Risk: API rate limits → Mitigation: Cache responses and use exponential back-off for retries.
•        Risk: Feed schema changes → Mitigation: Implement source-specific adapters and validation.
•        Risk: Network or source downtime → Mitigation: Gracefully skip unavailable sources and alert via logs or dashboards.

column4: As a user, I want duplicate articles removed so that I don’t see the same story repeated.
column7:
1.        Given two or more fetched articles with identical headlines and content, When the aggregation service processes them, Then only one article is stored and displayed.
2.        Given duplicates differ slightly in metadata (e.g., summary length, source), When merging occurs, Then the system keeps the version with the most complete metadata (e.g., full text, images, publication date).
column9:
•        Article fetching service (from “Fetch News Sources”).
•        Hashing or similarity algorithm (e.g., text hashing, cosine similarity).
•        Storage layer that supports unique constraints or merging logic.
column10:
•        Risk: Over-aggressive deduplication removes distinct stories → Mitigation: Tune similarity threshold and review with sample datasets.
•        Risk: Slightly reformatted articles bypass deduplication → Mitigation: Use normalized text (e.g., lowercase, punctuation stripped) and fuzzy matching.
•        Risk: Increased processing time with large datasets → Mitigation: Batch deduplication asynchronously with queue processing.

column4: As a user, I want articles categorized by topic so that I can browse news that interests me.
column7:
1.        Given new articles are fetched, When the content aggregation pipeline processes them, Then each article is automatically assigned at least one topic tag (e.g., “Politics,” “Tech,” “Health”).
2.        Given the user views the content list, When they apply a topic filter, Then only articles matching that category are displayed.
column9:
•        Natural language processing (NLP) or keyword-based classification model.
•        Taxonomy or topic schema definition.
•        Front-end filtering UI and API endpoints supporting topic queries.
column10:
•        Risk: Misclassification of topics due to model inaccuracy → Mitigation: Allow manual tag correction or feedback loop for retraining.
•        Risk: Topic schema changes cause inconsistencies → Mitigation: Maintain a versioned taxonomy and migration script.
•        Risk: Filtering performance lag with large datasets → Mitigation: Use indexed fields and pagination on queries.

column4: As a user, I want to see publication timestamps so that I know how current an article is.
column7:
1.        Given an article is fetched, When it is stored, Then its publication date and time are extracted from the source metadata.
2.        Given a list of articles is displayed, When they render on screen, Then they are ordered by recency (newest first) and each shows its publication timestamp.
3.        Given a source does not provide a timestamp, When the article is displayed, Then the system substitutes the fetch time and indicates “retrieved on [date/time].”
column9:
•        Article fetching and parsing pipeline.
•        Consistent time zone handling (e.g., UTC normalization).
•        UI display component for date/time formatting.
column10:
•        Risk: Missing or inconsistent timestamp fields → Mitigation: Implement fallback to fetch time and standardize date parsing.
•        Risk: Cross-time-zone confusion → Mitigation: Normalize to UTC in backend and convert to local time on front-end.
•        Risk: Sorting errors if timestamps are strings → Mitigation: Store timestamps as ISO datetime objects for consistent comparison.

column4: N/A
column7: N/A

column4: As a user, I want tailored versions of articles so that I can understand complex topics at my current level of knowledge.
column7:
1.        Given a user has an existing reading history, When they access a new article, Then the system selects a version tailored to their familiarity level (e.g., beginner vs expert).
2.        Given a user frequently reads on the same topic, When a new article on that topic is displayed, Then the version shown omits overly basic explanations and focuses on updates or deeper insights.
3.        Given an anonymous or new user without history, When they open an article, Then the system defaults to the beginner-level version.
column9:
•        User profile and reading history tracking service.
•        AI/NLP summarization or re-writing model to generate simplified or expert-level variants.
•        Content caching for pre-generated versions.
column10:
•        Risk: Tailoring model misjudges user expertise → Mitigation: Allow users to manually override level (via “Switch Levels”).
•        Risk: High compute cost from on-demand AI generation → Mitigation: Cache generated versions and batch-preprocess popular topics.
•        Risk: Bias in model training → Mitigation: Use diverse training datasets and continuous evaluation.

column4: As a user, I want to switch between complexity levels (beginner/intermediate/expert) so that I can choose the detail I need.
column7:
1.        Given an article with multiple complexity levels, When the user selects a different level, Then the displayed text updates instantly without reloading the entire page.
2.        Given a user switches levels, When the new text loads, Then the system preserves their scroll position and highlighted terms.
3.        Given an article has level-specific summaries, When switching occurs, Then the system ensures factual consistency across all levels (no contradictions or missing data).
column9:
•        Tailored content generation (from “Tailor Versions”).
•        Front-end UI component for level toggling.
•        State management system for maintaining context (scroll position, highlights).
column10:
•        Risk: Latency in switching between levels → Mitigation: Pre-load alternate levels or use background fetching.
•        Risk: Content drift (different levels conveying inconsistent facts) → Mitigation: Validate level variants with factual consistency checks.
•        Risk: Accessibility issues with switch controls → Mitigation: Implement keyboard and screen-reader support.

column4: As a user, I want technical terms highlighted so that I can easily spot unfamiliar words.
column7:
1.        Given an article is displayed, When the system detects jargon or specialized terms, Then those terms are visually highlighted with consistent styling (e.g., color, underline).
2.        Given a user reads through the text, When they encounter highlighted words, Then no disruptive formatting (e.g., large popups or font shifts) occurs that breaks reading flow.
3.        Given multiple occurrences of the same term, When the article renders, Then the highlight format remains consistent throughout.
column9:
•        NLP keyword extraction or domain terminology database.
•        Front-end rendering system for inline formatting.
•        Accessibility and theme guidelines (for color contrast and readability).
column10:
•        Risk: Over-highlighting common words → Mitigation: Filter out stopwords and context-irrelevant matches.
•        Risk: Visual clutter in dense technical articles → Mitigation: Allow toggle to show/hide highlights.
•        Risk: Style inconsistency across browsers → Mitigation: Define highlight style in a shared CSS component.

column4: As a user, I want definitions shown in tooltips/popups so that I can learn without leaving the article.
column7:
1.        Given an article contains highlighted technical terms, When the user hovers over or taps the term, Then a tooltip or popup displays a concise definition.
2.        Given the definition popup is open, When the user continues reading, Then the popup remains non-intrusive and can be dismissed easily.
3.        Given a term appears multiple times, When any occurrence is hovered, Then the same definition content is displayed for consistency.
4.        Given a term lacks a definition in the system, When the user hovers over it, Then the tooltip states “Definition unavailable” or hides the highlight gracefully.
column9:
•        Highlighting service (from “Highlight Technical Terms”).
•        Knowledge graph or glossary API for retrieving term definitions.
•        UI framework for non-blocking tooltips (e.g., React Portal, Popper.js).
column10:
•        Risk: Tooltip positioning issues on mobile → Mitigation: Use responsive placement and fallback to inline expansion.
•        Risk: Missing or inaccurate definitions → Mitigation: Maintain versioned glossary and human review process for common terms.
•        Risk: Tooltip load lag → Mitigation: Pre-fetch definitions during article render or lazy-load in background.

column4: N/A
column7: N/A

column4: As a user, I want to select my preferred topics so that I get relevant news.
column7:
1.        Given a new user during onboarding, When they are prompted to choose topics, Then they can select one or more from a predefined list (e.g., “Technology,” “Politics,” “Health”).
2.        Given a returning user, When they navigate to settings, Then they can update, add, or remove topic preferences at any time.
3.        Given topic preferences are updated, When changes are saved, Then the system immediately applies them to future recommendations and content feeds.
column9:
•	Topic taxonomy and tagging system (from “Categorize Content”).
•	User profile and settings storage.
•	UI components for topic selection (checkboxes, chips, etc.).
column10:
•        Risk: Too many or confusing topic options → Mitigation: Use clear labels and group related topics.
•        Risk: Preference updates not syncing to backend → Mitigation: Implement confirmation messages and API success callbacks.
•        Risk: Users skip onboarding and miss personalization → Mitigation: Provide in-app reminders or quick settings shortcuts.

column4: As a user, I want recommendations based on my reading history so that I discover new articles.
column7:
1.        Given a user has a reading history, When they view an article, Then the system displays 3–5 recommended articles related by topic, author, or reading pattern.
2.        Given a user opens the homepage or dashboard, When they scroll through their feed, Then the system interleaves personalized recommendations among new content.
3.        Given new reading activity, When the system refreshes recommendations, Then the updated suggestions appear below or beside the current article.
column9:
•        Recommendation model using content similarity or collaborative filtering.
•        Reading history tracking.
•        Display layout for recommended articles (e.g., sidebar, carousel).
column10:
•        Risk: Cold-start problem for new users with no history → Mitigation: Fall back to trending or topic-based recommendations.
•        Risk: Recommendations become repetitive or narrow → Mitigation: Diversify algorithm by introducing exploration factors (e.g., 10–20% novel content).
•        Risk: Latency fetching recommendations → Mitigation: Precompute results and cache by user ID.

column4: As a user, I want to give feedback on recommendations so that the system improves future suggestions.
column7:
1.        Given a user views recommended articles, When they click “useful” or “not useful,” Then the feedback is captured and associated with their profile.
2.        Given the feedback data, When the system updates the recommendation model, Then future article suggestions reflect this input (e.g., fewer similar “not useful” items).
3.        Given the feedback is submitted, When the system receives confirmation from the backend, Then the UI immediately acknowledges the response (e.g., via icon state or toast message).
column9:
•        Recommendation engine with feedback weighting capability.
•        User interaction logging and analytics.
•        UI buttons or gestures for feedback.
column10:
•        Risk: Feedback not reflected quickly → Mitigation: Schedule nightly model retraining or incremental updates.
•        Risk: Users give conflicting feedback → Mitigation: Implement decay weighting to favor recent feedback.
•        Risk: Feedback fatigue reduces participation → Mitigation: Keep UI minimal and optional, with lightweight animations or badges.

column4: As a user, I don’t want to be recommended articles I’ve already read so that my feed stays fresh.
column7:
1.        Given a user’s reading history, When the system generates new recommendations, Then any article IDs or URLs already read are excluded.
2.        Given the user revisits the same session, When new recommendations load, Then previously clicked or dismissed articles do not reappear.
3.        Given a large recommendation dataset, When deduplication runs, Then the system ensures unique and fresh results for each session.
column9:
•        Reading history and recommendation model.
•        Deduplication logic from content aggregation.
•        Session tracking to remember recent article IDs.
column10:
•        Risk: Incomplete tracking due to logout or privacy settings → Mitigation: Store local read cache in browser/session storage.
•        Risk: Low recommendation pool causes repeats → Mitigation: Expand source pool and enforce minimum content freshness window.
•        Risk: Users lose interest if feed stagnates → Mitigation: Mix “new for you” and trending sections.

column4: N/A
column7: N/A

column4: As a user, I want to search by keyword so that I can find specific topics.
column7:
1.        Given the user enters a keyword or phrase in the search bar, When they submit the query, Then matching results appear within 2 seconds.
2.        Given search results are displayed, When they appear, Then each item shows a title, source, and snippet of content.
3.        Given no results are found, When the query returns empty, Then the system displays a “No results found” message with related suggestions.
column9:
•        Search indexing service or database with full-text search.
•        Query parser and ranking algorithm.
•        Cached indexing from content aggregation.
column10:
•        Risk: Slow search response times → Mitigation: Use pre-indexed fields and async search results.
•        Risk: Irrelevant ranking or poor query matching → Mitigation: Implement fuzzy search and weight by recency.
•        Risk: Heavy API load under many concurrent searches → Mitigation: Add caching and rate limiting.

column4: As a user, I want to filter by source so that I can read from publishers I trust.
column7:
1.        Given the search results are displayed, When the user selects one or more sources, Then the list filters instantly without a full page reload.
2.        Given no source is selected, When the filter is cleared, Then all available sources reappear.
3.        Given the selected source has no results, When the filter applies, Then a message informs the user that no articles match.
column9:
•        Source metadata tagging from content aggregation.
•        Front-end filtering logic.
•        Dynamic query builder in backend search API.
column10:
•        Risk: Filters not syncing with search results → Mitigation: Maintain unified search state store (frontend).
•        Risk: Empty results misinterpreted as failure → Mitigation: Display clear empty-state messaging.
•        Risk: Source names inconsistent across feeds → Mitigation: Normalize and map publisher identifiers.

column4: As a user, I want to filter by date so that I can find the latest news.
column7:
1.        Given the user opens the date filter, When they select a date range, Then only articles published within that range display.
2.        Given the filter is active, When the user adjusts the range, Then the results update instantly.
3.        Given the user clears the date filter, When they confirm, Then the feed resets to show all articles.
column9:
•        Article timestamps from content aggregation.
•        Front-end date picker UI component.
•        Backend query logic to filter by timestamp fields.
column10:
•        Risk: Inconsistent time zones in data → Mitigation: Normalize all timestamps to UTC.
•        Risk: Large date ranges cause performance lag → Mitigation: Paginate and pre-fetch incremental results.
•        Risk: UX confusion when filters combine → Mitigation: Show active filter chips and reset options.

column4: As a user, I want trending topics suggested so that I can see what’s popular.
column7:
1.        Given user visits the homepage or search page, When the trending module loads, Then it displays the top 5 most-read topics.
        2.        Given system logs article views, When new data accumulates, Then trending topics update at least once per hour.
        3.        Given two topics tie in popularity, When displayed, Then both appear ranked equally or alphabetically.
column9:
•        Analytics service tracking article views and topic tags.
•        Aggregation job to calculate trending metrics.
•        UI widget to display and refresh list.
column10:
•        Risk: Inaccurate trending data due to caching delay → Mitigation: Update hourly via background job.
•        Risk: Popularity bias (only large sources dominate) → Mitigation: Weight by user diversity and unique readers.
•        Risk: Overlapping trends clutter UI → Mitigation: Cap list to 5 and collapse similar keywords.

column4: N/A
column7: N/A

column4: As a user, I want to give quick Yes/No feedback on simplified articles so that the system can improve how it adjusts content for my reading level.
column7:
1.        Given a simplified article is displayed, When the user selects “Yes” or “No” on the clarity prompt, Then the response is recorded with a timestamp.
2.        Given a “No” response, When submitted, Then the system suggests switching to a different complexity level.
3.        Given the feedback is stored, When linked to the user profile, Then it contributes to future tailoring model updates.
column9:
•        Tailored content service and reading level model.
•        Feedback API and database table for clarity responses.
•        Front-end component for Yes/No prompt.
column10:
•        Risk: Feedback ignored by model retraining → Mitigation: Include regular retraining job validation.
•        Risk: UI fatigue from repeated prompts → Mitigation: Show feedback widget only every few articles.
•        Risk: Data privacy issues → Mitigation: Anonymize or aggregate feedback logs.

column4: As a user, I want to flag unclear definitions so that the system can improve the quality of explanations.
column7:
1.        Given a tooltip definition appears, When the user selects “Flag as unclear,” Then the system stores the flag with user ID and timestamp.
2.        Given the feedback is logged, When admins access the dashboard, Then they can review flagged terms for quality assurance.
3.        Given a flag is submitted, When recorded successfully, Then the user sees a confirmation message.
column9:
•        Tooltip/definition service.
•        Feedback storage and admin dashboard.
•        Authentication for admin review portal.
column10:
•        Risk: Too many duplicate flags on same term → Mitigation: Aggregate identical reports.
•        Risk: Low follow-up on feedback → Mitigation: Assign internal review cadence.
•        Risk: Sensitive user data in logs → Mitigation: Store anonymized IDs.

column4: As a platform administrator, I want all feedback to be logged and used to retrain AI models so that article simplifications and definitions continuously improve.
column7:
1.        Given feedback is collected from clarity ratings and flagged definitions, When the system exports logs, Then each record includes feedback type, timestamp, and anonymized user ID.
2.        Given the retraining schedule runs (e.g., weekly), When data is available, Then AI models are retrained using the latest feedback.
3.        Given retraining completes, When new models deploy, Then version numbers update in system logs.
column9:
•        Feedback logging pipelines.
•        Model training infrastructure (batch retraining scripts).
•        Secure storage for anonymized data exports.
column10:
•        Risk: Model drift or degraded quality after retraining → Mitigation: Evaluate new models on validation dataset before deployment.
•        Risk: Feedback volume too low for meaningful updates → Mitigation: Implement synthetic data augmentation.
•        Risk: Data privacy compliance → Mitigation: Strip identifiers before export and document policies.

column4: N/A
column7: N/A

column4: As a user, I want to subscribe to specific topics so that I receive alerts for news that interests me.
column7:
1.        Given the user opens the topic subscription page, When they select at least three preferred topics, Then those are saved to their profile.
2.        Given the user updates subscriptions, When changes are made, Then the updated list persists immediately.
3.        Given alerts are generated, When a new article matches a subscribed topic, Then only those topics trigger notifications.
column9:
•        Topic taxonomy and user preference storage.
•        Notification service (push/email).
•        Backend subscription manager.
column10:
•        Risk: Users overwhelmed by alerts → Mitigation: Set frequency limits or digest mode.
•        Risk: Subscription sync errors → Mitigation: Confirm save with UI acknowledgment.
•        Risk: Backend filtering errors → Mitigation: Test query matching thoroughly.

column4: As a user, I want to receive breaking news alerts within my subscribed topics so that I stay updated in real time.
column7:
1.        Given an article is flagged as breaking news within a subscribed topic, When it’s published, Then the system sends alerts within 5 minutes.
2.        Given a user has a preferred alert method, When an alert is triggered, Then it’s sent via push or email accordingly.
3.        Given the same event has multiple updates, When an alert has already been sent, Then duplicates are suppressed.
column9:
•        Real-time monitoring of feeds (RSS/API).
•        Notification queue and delivery system.
•        Deduplication logic for breaking news events.
column10:
•        Risk: Delay in push/email delivery → Mitigation: Prioritize notifications with lightweight payloads.
•        Risk: Duplicate notifications → Mitigation: Use event ID tracking.
•        Risk: Misclassification of “breaking” → Mitigation: Define clear detection rules or editorial input.

column4: As a user, I want to manage my news alerts so that I only receive notifications that are relevant to me.
column7:
1.        Given the user navigates to alert settings, When they disable a topic, Then no further notifications for that topic are sent.
2.        Given delivery method options exist, When the user selects email, push, or none, Then those preferences apply instantly.
3.        Given the settings are changed, When saved, Then the system confirms updates with a success message.
column9:
•        Notification preference management service.
•        User profile synchronization.
•        Push/email delivery integrations.
column10:
•        Risk: Preferences not persisting → Mitigation: Implement backend confirmation endpoint.
•        Risk: Push notifications blocked by device → Mitigation: Detect permission status and guide user.
•        Risk: Inconsistent alert timing across devices → Mitigation: Sync via centralized notification queue.
